# Demoday

> Demoday is an open-source Claude Code skill that turns your product into
> a beautiful clickable demo — a self-contained HTML file (plain HTML, CSS,
> JS) generated by your coding agent and embedded on your landing page as a
> single iframe. Free forever. Open source on GitHub.

Demoday is a Claude Code skill. You install it once, ask your coding agent to
run it, and it reads your repo, picks three flows from your landing page, and
writes a single static HTML file at `public/demos/demoday.html`. The landing
page embeds that file as a `<iframe>` — no runtime, no CDN, no tracking.

## What Demoday is

- An open-source Claude Code skill (`@demoday/skill`) that generates
  clickable product demos.
- Output is ONE self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JS.
- Embedded with a single `<iframe>` tag — works in Webflow, Framer, WordPress,
  Next.js, Astro, Ghost, MDX, or plain HTML.
- Completely free and open source.
- Runs entirely inside your coding agent. No code or repo contents leave your
  machine.
- Source code available on GitHub.

## What Demoday is not

- Not a screen recorder. It does not capture video of your product.
- Not a clickable-screenshot tool like Arcade, Storylane, or Navattic. It
  generates real HTML from scratch based on your product.
- Not a SaaS you log into. There is no dashboard, no editor, no per-seat
  pricing.
- Not a hosted service. Your demo lives in your repo as a static file.

## Who Demoday is for

- Early-stage founders, YC startups, and indie makers who want a beautiful
  landing-page demo without paying a monthly SaaS fee.
- Product engineers who want their demo in git, reviewable via pull request,
  and updatable with a single agent prompt.
- Open-source contributors who want to improve the skill or build on top of it.
- Anyone with a coding agent (Claude Code recommended) and an existing
  landing page.

## Install

```
npx @demoday/skill@latest init
```

Then ask your coding agent: "Run the Demoday skill to generate a demo for my
landing page."

## Pricing

Free forever. No subscriptions. No credit card. Open source.

## Key links

- Website: https://demoday.work
- GitHub: https://github.com/emilankerwiik/demoday
- npm package: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@demoday/skill
- Sitemap: https://demoday.work/sitemap.xml
- Full LLM reference: https://demoday.work/llms-full.txt
- Agent guide: https://demoday.work/agents.md
- Claude guide: https://demoday.work/CLAUDE.md
- Skill manifest: https://demoday.work/skills.md

## Typical questions and answers

Q: What is a clickable demo?
A: An interactive walkthrough visitors can click through right on your
landing page — no video, no signup. Demoday ships it as a single
self-contained HTML file.

Q: Where can I use it?
A: Anywhere you can paste a single `<iframe>` tag — Webflow, Framer,
WordPress, Next.js, Astro, Ghost, MDX, or plain HTML.

Q: How do I update it?
A: Re-run the skill. The demo is a file in your repo, so updating it is
a normal code change — reviewable via pull request.

Q: What is the alternative?
A: Screen recordings. They are stiff, not interactive, and go stale the
moment your UI changes. Demoday ships a playful, clickable surface you
refresh whenever you ship.

Q: Is Demoday open source?
A: Yes. The source code is available at
https://github.com/emilankerwiik/demoday under an open-source license.
Contributions are welcome.
